items.title: 2024 Research Forecast Report: Global Healthcare Cognitive Computing Market Projected to Reach $150.25 Billion By 2035 from $10.81 Billion in 2024
items.author: Researchandmarkets.Com
items.description: The global healthcare cognitive computing market is expected to grow from $10.81 billion in 2024 to $150.25 billion by 2035, driven by the adoption of cloud services, increase in chronic diseases, and the need for advanced patient data analysis.

items.title: Data Center Construction Market Growth Forecast (2024-2032) by Infrastructure, Tier Type, Size, End Use and Region
items.author: Researchandmarkets.Com
items.description: The global data center construction market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 7.2% from 2024 to 2032, driven by the increasing demand for cloud computing, big data analytics, and IoT applications. The market is expected to reach a value of USD 123.82 billion by 2032.

items.title: Should You Forget MicroStrategy and Buy 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks Instead?
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article discusses the risks associated with MicroStrategy's focus on Bitcoin and suggests two AI stocks, IBM and Micron, as potentially better investment options due to their strong positioning in the AI market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: MicroStrategy's stock is swaddled in risks due to its all-in focus on Bitcoin, which could be negatively impacted by a drop in Bitcoin prices.
IBM's AI expertise is unrivaled, and its upcoming mainframe systems with AI accelerator chips are expected to drive growth in the enterprise-class AI market. The stock also appears undervalued.

Micron is a leading provider of high-speed memory and storage chips that are crucial for AI systems. The company is expanding its manufacturing capacity to meet the surging demand, and its stock appears to be a bargain compared to forward-looking metrics.
